# Searcher One-Pager Reference Research

**Author:** Multi-source web research
**Source:** [Web research synthesis — see table below for individual URLs](https://www.permanentequity.com/investment-criteria)
**Saved:** 2026-05-04

## Why I Saved This
Reference material for G&B's broker-channel one-pager build. Studying structure, voice, and section-by-section design choices from 10 published mandates across the search-fund + small-holdco landscape.

## Content

### Reference Table

| # | Firm / Searcher | Source URL | Closed deal? | Section structure (in order) | "How funded" framing | EBITDA / revenue criteria | Industry framing | Founder bio | Voice standout |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Permanent Equity** (Brent Beshore) | [permanentequity.com/investment-criteria](https://www.permanentequity.com/investment-criteria) | **Y** — multi-portfolio, well-documented (>$300M AUM, Fund I 2017, Fund II 2019 $248M) | 4 deal-type buckets (Growth Partnership / Legacy Buyout / Legacy Transition / Other) — each with Life Stage, DFCF, Revenue & Profitability, Situation | "30-year committed funds" — no LP names, no AUM published on this page | DFCF $2-25M+, Revenue $5-250M, "double-digit net operating margins" | Industry-agnostic; explicitly excludes commodity / trend-reliant | Minimal — Emily Holdman named, single email | "strict no assholes policy"; tagline "companies that care what happens next" — values-driven, anti-PE tone |
| 2 | **Chenmark** (Higgins family) | [chenmark.com](https://chenmark.com/) | **Y** — ~30+ acquisitions across 7 platforms, ~450 employees | Hero ("Chasing Better Every Day") then "Selling Your Business" empathy section then 3 testimonials then contact | Family-business / self-funded compounding ("cash flows from co. 1 buy co. 2"); no LPs, no AUM | Not published on landing — known externally as $1-3M EBITDA | Industry-agnostic; "boring, slow-growth, steady" positioning (off-page) | Family-led — emphasizes family business + multi-generational mindset | Seller-empathy tone ("Selling your business isn't easy"); testimonial-heavy, criteria-light |
| 3 | **HOLD.co** | [hold.co](https://hold.co/) | **Y** — published portfolio showcase | Hero then 4 vertical pillars then real-estate emphasis then FAQ then portfolio | "operator-led acquisition platform… not a traditional PE firm… do not raise blind-pool funds or pursue short-term flips" — explicit anti-PE | "$2 million or more in EBITDA"; no ceiling | **Highly specific 4 verticals**: Manufacturing / Industrial Services / Logistics / Equipment-Intensive B2B | Minimal — "team in five countries" only | Triple-repetition conviction ("real facilities, real equipment, real customers"); "asset-heavy," "durable, cash-producing" |
| 4 | **Inu Capital** (Noah Smith) | [inucapital.com](https://www.inucapital.com) | N (founded 2023, no closed deals on record) | Hero then Our Approach then Investment Criteria then Team/Contact links | "operator-led investment firm" — Axial profile mentions "family offices and successful operators with well over $1b combined assets" but NOT on the criteria page itself | Revenue $5-50M; EBITDA/FCF $1-7M; EV up to $50M; recurring revenue + history of profitability | "Business-services industry" (single-vertical commitment) | Off-page (Team page) — Noah Smith bio emphasizes 3x founding CEO + middle-market PE deal experience | Clean, no fluff; tight bullet criteria; commitment-to-stakeholders line |
| 5 | **Fourfold Ventures** (Ben) | [fourfoldventures.co](https://www.fourfoldventures.co/) | N (founded late 2024, searching) | Hero (criteria-forward) — minimal site | Self-funded search firm — explicit | EBITDA $750k-$2M; 10+ employees; some management in place | B2B / B2G services / non-discretionary B2C / manufacturing | Founder bio emphasizes CFA + Raymond James fixed-income trader background + Nebraska MBA | Self-funded transparency; tight target description |
| 6 | **Ironia Holdings** (Shawn Nuttall) | [ironiaholdings.com](https://ironiaholdings.com/) | N (early-stage searcher) | Mission then "What we're seeking" then Geography then Founder | Funding source NOT described on page (omission noteworthy) | EBITDA $750k+; "several years of stable, positive cash flow" | In-scope: services / mfg / distribution. Out-of-scope explicitly listed: retail, restaurants, startups, turnarounds. Geo: NJ/VA/NC/MD/DE | ~50 words — operational depth (engineering, supply chain, automation, AI) — no personal narrative | "Step into the daily leadership role, preserving the culture, team, and reputation" — stewardship language; explicit out-of-scope list is rare and useful |
| 7 | **Reef Point Management** (Navy vet, Wharton MBA) | [reefpointmanagement.com](https://www.reefpointmanagement.com/) | Y (founder claims "three small businesses" acquired, but unnamed) | Hero w/ founder photo then free-consultation CTA then mission then 3 pillars | None published — appears sole-operator | NOT published — no EBITDA/revenue thresholds | **Single-niche specialist** — Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) — regulatory classification, not industry vertical | Brief — Navy Vet + Wharton MBA + serial entrepreneur, equal weighting | Veteran-to-veteran tone; coaching offered "at no cost" — gives away advice as on-ramp |
| 8 | **Roslyn Ridge Holdings** (Philadelphia) | [roslynridgeholdings.com](https://www.roslynridgeholdings.com/) | N (founded 2023, 0 investments per PitchBook) | Standard searcher template | Not published | EBITDA $750k-$3.5M (adjusted) | Mid-Atlantic; manufacturing / logistics / tech services / **defense-adjacent** | Standard | "Defense-adjacent" framing is an unusual sub-vertical lens worth noting |
| 9 | **SummitView Holdings** (Hatfield, PA) | [axial.net/company/summitview-holdings](https://www.axial.net/company/summitview-holdings/) | N (no closed deals) | Axial-profile hosted | Not published | Lower middle-market; specifics not published | "**Mission-critical services or solutions required to be carried out at regular intervals across all economic cycles**" within Industrials / Light Mfg / Business Services | Standard | Best phrase in the set — "mission-critical… required at regular intervals across all economic cycles" frames recession-resistance positively |
| 10 | **Llama Capital Partners** (Stephen Kuhl + Kabeer Chopra) | [llamacapital.partners](https://www.llamacapital.partners) | **Y as operators** (built Burrow to $100M+ rev exit to Havenly Brands) — first acquisition under Llama not yet confirmed | Hero then Our story (Burrow exit) then Approach then Team | No fund structure described — operator-led | Not published as numbers; "perennial businesses" qualitative | "Customer-first playbook"; not vertical-specific | **Founder bio is the lead asset** — Burrow $100M+ exit is the entire credibility play | Operator-credibility-first design — bio leads, criteria trail. Notable inversion of typical structure |

## Key Patterns

- **Pattern 1 — No one names specific LP firms.** Across all 10, zero published documents name backing investors. Closest disclosure is Inu Capital ("family offices and successful operators with $1b+ combined assets") but only on Axial profile, not on its own site. Permanent Equity discloses fund vintage/size in third-party press but not on the criteria page itself. **Implication for G&B: do NOT name the 12 LPs in the broker-facing one-pager. "Backed by family offices and operators" or "long-term committed capital" is the ceiling of disclosure norm.**

- **Pattern 2 — Two structural archetypes dominate.** (a) **Criteria-forward** (Permanent Equity, HOLD.co, Inu, Fourfold, Ironia) — sections lead with what they buy. (b) **Story-forward** (Llama, Chenmark, Reef Point) — founder credibility leads, criteria follow. **The credible operators (Llama Burrow exit, Chenmark family compounder) earn story-forward; first-time searchers default to criteria-forward.** Kay's G&B has personal credibility (operator background) — story-forward is on the table.

- **Pattern 3 — Margin floors are universal, ceilings are absent.** Every one-pager that publishes margins states a floor (15%+, "double-digit") — none state a ceiling. **Mirrors Kay's `feedback_deal_screen_300k_salary_15pct_margin` doctrine.** Aligned with the broader pattern that margin floors are credibility, ceilings would advertise pickiness.

- **Pattern 4 — Industry framing splits 3 ways: agnostic / multi-vertical / single-vertical.** (i) Agnostic = Permanent Equity + Chenmark (earned credibility lets them be vague). (ii) Multi-vertical = HOLD.co (4 named pillars), Roslyn Ridge (4 sectors), Fourfold (4 sectors), SummitView (3 sectors). (iii) Single-vertical = Inu (business services), Reef Point (SDVOSB). **For G&B's broker channel — where industry-agnostic is the explicit positioning per `feedback_broker_channel_opportunistic_floor` — the Permanent Equity / Chenmark agnostic model is closest fit, but G&B doesn't have their portfolio credibility yet, so a SummitView-style "mission-critical services across economic cycles" framing may be the better bridge.**

- **Pattern 5 — Geography handling matches Kay's INTERNAL-only doctrine.** Of the 10, only Ironia and Roslyn Ridge publish geography on their own site. Everyone else either omits or lists nationally. Validates `feedback_geography_not_in_external_broker_copy` — most credible firms don't publish geo restrictions in external copy. **G&B should keep NY/NJ/PA/CT internal.**

- **Pattern 6 — Out-of-scope language is rare and high-value.** Only Ironia explicitly lists exclusions ("retail, restaurants, startups, turnarounds"). Permanent Equity does it gently ("not reliant on a commodity, or recent trends"). Most are silent. **Borrow this — an explicit "not for us" line saves broker triage time and signals discipline.** Aligns with G&B's no-California, no-lending, no-carve-outs filters.

- **Pattern 7 — Founder bios skew minimal except where exit credibility exists.** Ironia (~50 words), Inu (off-page), Permanent Equity (single name + email). Llama is the exception — Burrow exit IS the pitch. **For G&B, Kay's operator background is real but doesn't yet carry Burrow-level credibility — a 50-100 word bio emphasizing operator depth + relevant past wins (not full CV) matches the dominant pattern.**

## How I Might Use This

### Borrow for G&B
1. **SummitView's "mission-critical… required at regular intervals across all economic cycles"** phrase — exceptional framing of recession-resistance.
2. **Ironia's explicit out-of-scope list** — saves broker triage time.
3. **HOLD.co's anti-PE positioning** ("not a traditional PE firm, no blind-pool funds, no short-term flips") — directly maps to G&B's holdco-not-fund identity.
4. **Permanent Equity's 4-deal-type structure** (Growth Partnership / Legacy Buyout / Legacy Transition) — sophisticated framing that lets a single doc speak to multiple seller scenarios. Probably too much surface area for a one-pager but worth knowing.
5. **Generic "long-term committed capital" / "family offices and operators" language** — universal norm; matches Kay's intermediary-no-fund-language doctrine.

### Avoid for G&B
1. **Llama's story-forward / bio-leads structure** — Kay doesn't yet have a Burrow-scale exit to anchor this; defaulting to credentials over criteria reads as overreach. Revisit post-Deal-1.
2. **Reef Point's missing financial criteria** — leaves brokers with nothing to triage on; brokers want the buy-box up front.
3. **Chenmark's testimonial-heavy / criteria-light landing** — works because they have closed 30 deals; pre-deal-1 G&B can't ship this.
4. **Roslyn Ridge's "defense-adjacent"** language and similar trend-chasing sub-verticals — narrows surface area for an industry-agnostic broker channel.
5. **Naming any backing LP firm** — universally avoided in this set; reinforce existing doctrine.

### Open question for Kay
Of the two dominant structures (criteria-forward like HOLD.co/Inu vs. balanced like Permanent Equity), which fits G&B's broker channel? **Recommendation: criteria-forward** — brokers triage on buy-box, not on operator narrative. Operator narrative belongs in the warm-intro / direct-outreach one-pager (a separate asset).
